summary: Exhaustively map the new contact-page handoff, the existing contact stack, design tokens, and the motion system + static-page structure to ground an integrated design

handoff.states_and_toggle:
TWO STATES, toggled by display + a class — never unmounted.

- Form view: `<main class="gt-main" id="gt-form-view">` (default visible). 
- Success view: `<section class="gt-thanks" id="gt-thanks-view" aria-live="polite">` — CSS `.gt-thanks { display: none; }`, becomes visible via `.gt-thanks.is-active { display: flex; }`.

Toggle logic (vanilla JS, inside the form `submit` handler, AFTER validation passes):
```
formView.style.display = 'none';            // hide form (inline style)
thanksView.classList.add('is-active');      // show success (CSS flips display:none -> flex)
window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'auto' });
fireConfetti();
```
There is NO reverse toggle in the reference (no "send another"). State is one-way: form -> success. README frames it as a single boolean `submitted` (false=form, true=success). In Astro/React port: keep both subtrees mounted, gate with the boolean, and DO NOT conditionally unmount the success node before animating (transform-only animations need it present + visible).

Both views live inside the shared dark wrapper `<div class="gt-page" id="gt-page">` which also holds the two glow divs and the nav, so the dark gradient/glows persist across both states. The confetti `<canvas id="gt-confetti">` is a SIBLING of `.gt-page` (direct child of body), fixed-positioned over everything.
handoff.layout: SOURCE-OF-TRUTH NOTE: the real markup/CSS lives base64/JSON-encoded inside `contact-reference.html` as a `

`.gt-left { display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap: var(--space-5) (24px); }` EYEBROW (dot + label): `Get in touch` - `.gt-eyebrow { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:10px; font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size:12px; letter-spacing:0.18em; text-transform:uppercase; font-weight:700; color: rgba(251,246,238,0.6); }` - `.gt-eyebrow__dot { width:9px; height:9px; border-radius:50%; background: var(--pop-butter) (#E8C465); display:inline-block; }` HEADLINE: `

` + `

CARD `.gt-card`: `background: var(--cream-soft) (#FBF6EE); border-radius:24px; padding: var(--space-6) (32px); box-shadow: 0 30px 80px rgba(0,0,0,0.45); position:relative;` FORM `.gt-form#gt-form` with `novalidate`: `display:grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: var(--space-3) (12px);` `.gt-form .full { grid-column: 1 / -1; }` (full-width children) HEADER ROW `.gt-form-head` (full width): - `grid-column:1/-1; display:flex; align-items:baseline; justify-content:space-between; gap:var(--space-3); margin-bottom:var(--space-2)(8px); padding-bottom:var(--space-3)(12px); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rule);` - Left `Send us a note`: `font-family:var(--font-mono); font-size:11px; letter-spacing:0.16em; text-transform:uppercase; font-weight:700; color: var(--pop-magenta,#C44569);` - Right `We read every one`: `font-family:var(--font-mono); font-size:10px; letter-spacing:0.08em; color: var(--ink-mute) (rgba(31,26,35,0.42)); text-transform:uppercase; font-weight:600;` INPUTS `.gt-field`: `width:100%; box-sizing:border-box; border:1px solid var(--rule)(rgba(31,26,35,0.10)); border-radius:12px; background:white; color:var(--ink)(#1F1A23); font-family:var(--font-body); font-size:15px; padding:13px 15px; transition: border-color 160ms, box-shadow 160ms;` - `.gt-field::placeholder { color: var(--ink-mute); }` - `.gt-field:focus { outline:none; border-color: var(--pop-magenta,#C44569); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--pop-magenta,#C44569) 26%, transparent); }` (fallback rgba(196,69,105,0.26)) - `textarea.gt-field { resize:vertical; min-height:112px; }` - `select.gt-field { appearance:none; -webkit-appearance:none; cursor:pointer; background-image:url("data:image/svg+xml;utf8,"); background-repeat:no-repeat; background-position: right 15px center; padding-right:40px; }` (reference uses no actual ` | `` - "I am a…" segmented control (full width, see below) - `` - `` - Submit button (full width) "I AM A…" SEGMENTED CONTROL — `
`: - `.gt-seg { grid-column:1/-1; display:grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3,1fr); gap:8px; }` - Label `I am a…`: `grid-column:1/-1; font-family:var(--font-mono); font-size:11px; letter-spacing:0.14em; text-transform:uppercase; font-weight:700; color:var(--ink-mute); margin-bottom:2px;` - 3 segments, each ``: - `.gt-seg__opt { position:relative; }` - `.gt-seg__opt input { position:absolute; opacity:0; pointer-events:none; }` (visually-hidden radio) - `.gt-seg__opt span { display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; padding:12px 8px; border-radius:12px; cursor:pointer; border:1.5px solid var(--rule); background:white; font-family:var(--font-body); font-weight:600; font-size:14px; color:var(--ink-soft)(rgba(31,26,35,0.68)); text-align:center; transition: all 160ms; }` - `.gt-seg__opt:hover span { border-color: rgba(31,26,35,0.32); }` - CHECKED: `.gt-seg__opt input:checked + span { background: var(--ink)(#1F1A23); color: var(--cream-soft)(#FBF6EE); border-color: var(--ink); }` - `.gt-seg__opt input:focus-visible + span { box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, var(--pop-magenta,#C44569) 30%, transparent); }` - Options/values in order: Facility=`facility` (this one carries `required`), Clinician=`clinician`, Something else=`other`. SUBMIT BUTTON ``: - `.gt-submit { grid-column:1/-1; margin-top:var(--space-2)(8px); display:inline-flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; gap:10px; width:100%; border:none; cursor:pointer; background: var(--pop-magenta,#C44569); color: var(--cream-soft); font-family:var(--font-body); font-weight:700; font-size:16px; padding:16px 24px; border-radius:999px; transition: transform 160ms, box-shadow 160ms, background 160ms; }` - `.gt-submit:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 14px 34px rgba(196,69,105,0.45); }` - Puck: `.gt-submit__puck { width:28px; height:28px; border-radius:50%; background: var(--mn-black,#0A0A0F); color: var(--cream-soft); display:inline-flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; font-size:15px; transition: transform 160ms; }` - `.gt-submit:hover .gt-submit__puck { transform: translateX(3px); }` - Button label text is "Send it" (note: README narration says "Send button"; the literal glyph label is "Send it").

handoff.animation_timings:
ENTRANCE (only when `.gt-thanks.is-active` is present; all transform-only):
- Checkmark: `.gt-thanks.is-active .gt-thanks__mark { animation: gtPop 600ms var(--ease-snap); }` where `@keyframes gtPop { 0% { transform: scale(0.4); } 70% { transform: scale(1.12); } 100% { transform: scale(1); } }` and `--ease-snap: cubic-bezier(0.32,0.72,0.00,1)`.
- Title: `animation: gtRise 600ms var(--ease-out) 60ms backwards;`
- Body: `animation: gtRise 600ms var(--ease-out) 140ms backwards;`
- Signoff: `animation: gtRise 600ms var(--ease-out) 220ms backwards;`
- `@keyframes gtRise { from { transform: translateY(18px); } to { transform: translateY(0); } }` and `--ease-out: cubic-bezier(0.22,0.61,0.36,1)`.
- Staggered: title 60ms, body 140ms, signoff 220ms delays; all 600ms. `backwards` fill applies the start transform during the delay (start transform is translateY(18px) — a position offset, NOT opacity, so it is never invisible). The actions row has NO entrance animation.

GLOW DRIFT (ambient, infinite):
- `@keyframes gtFloatA { 0%,100% { transform: translate(0,0); } 50% { transform: translate(-30px, 26px); } }` on `.gt-glow--magenta`, 26s ease-in-out infinite.
- `@keyframes gtFloatB { 0%,100% { transform: translate(0,0); } 50% { transform: translate(26px, -22px); } }` on `.gt-glow--rose`, 30s ease-in-out infinite.

MICRO-TRANSITIONS (hover/focus): inputs border-color+box-shadow 160ms; segment span all 160ms; submit transform/box-shadow/background 160ms (hover translateY(-2px)); submit puck transform 160ms (hover translateX(3px)); thanks buttons transform/box-shadow/background 160ms (hover translateY(-2px)); info-value link color 180ms. Field focus ring transition 160ms. Validation shake: WAAPI translateX 0/-6/6/0 over 320ms.

REDUCED MOTION: only one rule in the gt block — `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .gt-glow { animation: none; } }`. The success entrance animations are intentionally NOT disabled because they are transform-only and their resting state is fully visible. The confetti is disabled inside fireConfetti() via the matchMedia early-return.

DUR/EASE TOKENS available: --dur-fast 150ms, --dur-base 280ms, --dur-slow 520ms; --ease-out cubic-bezier(0.22,0.61,0.36,1), --ease-snap cubic-bezier(0.32,0.72,0,1), --ease-spring cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1).
handoff.transform_only_gotcha: CRITICAL: every success-state entrance animation animates ONLY `transform` (scale or translateY) and its resting/0%-equivalent state is fully visible. There is NO `opacity:0 -> 1` anywhere, and no `animation-fill-mode` that leaves a hidden first frame. gtPop starts at scale(0.4) but is not opacity-gated; gtRise starts at translateY(18px) with `backwards` fill — still fully opaque. WHY: an element whose resting or first frame is `opacity:0` (or `scale:0`) becomes permanently INVISIBLE if animations are ever frozen or disabled — under prefers-reduced-motion, or during SSR/snapshot/thumbnail/print-capture paths where keyframes don't run. Because this is exactly such a path (the reference itself is captured as a static thumbnail), the success message and confetti would vanish if faded in. RULE for the port: keep the resting state fully visible; animate transform only. If you re-introduce a fade, gate it so the no-animation fallback resolves to `opacity:1` (e.g. default opacity:1, add the fade only behind `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)`), and never ship a bare `opacity:0` start frame on these nodes. This is also why the reduced-motion media query does NOT need to touch `.gt-thanks` — the transform-only design is self-safe; only `.gt-glow`'s ambient drift and the confetti are explicitly disabled.
handoff.placeholder_contact_info:
ALL contact details in this design are PLACEHOLDERS and must be replaced before go-live (they directly contradict the project's real, committed contact info):
- Email: `hello@imsstaffing.com` (mailto + visible text). REAL per project memory/committed content pass: `recruiting@iastaffing.com`. The "imsstaffing.com" domain itself looks invented.
- Phone: `(817) 555-0140` — a classic 555-01xx fictional/reserved number; href `tel:8175550140`. REAL committed number: `(512) 524-6686`. (Also area-code mismatch: 817 = Fort Worth, but the real line is 512 = Austin.)
- Office: "Fort Worth, TX" with meta "One floor, about 40 people". The "about 40 people" headcount is an unverified/invented stat (project memory flags headcount + the 200+/47 client counts as unconfirmed, pending Zach). City may be fine (prior memory references Fort Worth HQ) but treat the headcount line as placeholder copy.
- Meta lines "Replies within one business day" and "Mon to Fri, 8a to 6p Central" are plausible but unverified service claims — confirm hours before shipping.
- Nav/footer brand name "IMS / Innovative Medical Staffing" vs the real entity "IAS / iastaffing.com" — reconcile naming; signoff "the IMS team" should match whichever brand ships.
RECOMMENDATION: wire these to the same single source of truth the rest of the site already uses (the committed real contact details), do not hardcode the placeholders.
handoff.external_libs_or_assets:
NO external runtime libs required by the page itself — confetti is hand-rolled canvas, glyphs are text (`✓`, `→`), the select chevron is an inline data-URI SVG. README explicitly says "Assets: None required" and reuse the live nav logo.

FONTS (from the design system the reference links; map to the live site's existing families):
- Display: "Battlefin" (Pangram Pangram, PAID, self-hosted, upright weights 700–900) with fallback chain `"Battlefin","Fraunces","Migra",Georgia,serif`. Italic emphasis falls through to Fraunces via `--font-display-italic: "Fraunces","Migra",Georgia,serif` because Battlefin is upright-only — relevant for the italic signoff.
- Body: Inter — `"Inter","Inter Variable",system-ui,-apple-system,sans-serif`.
- Mono: JetBrains Mono — `"JetBrains Mono",ui-monospace,"SF Mono",Menlo,monospace`.
The decoded reference inlines Fraunces/Inter/JetBrains @font-face blocks (woff2) plus a Battlefin self-host; in the Astro port just reuse the site's already-loaded font stack/tokens — do not re-import.

CSS FEATURES USED (verify support / provide fallbacks): `color-mix(in srgb, …)` (page bg glows, focus rings — README gives rgba fallbacks: rgba(196,69,105,0.38)/(0.30 rose)/(0.26 focus)/(0.30 seg-focus)), `backdrop-filter: blur(14px)` (nav, with -webkit- prefix), `clamp()`, CSS grid, `Element.animate()` WAAPI (shake; guarded by `&&`), `matchMedia` (confetti). 

DESIGN TOKENS to hardcode or map (Magenta Noir effective palette — note the reference's base :root defines different hues that a later `!important` :root override replaces; these overridden values are what actually render):
`--pop-magenta/--mn-magenta: #C44569; --pop-rose/--mn-rose: #D88B9F; --pop-butter/--mn-butter: #E8C465; --ims-cyan/--mn-cyan: #59BFE7; --mn-black: #0A0A0F; --ink: #1F1A23; --ink-soft: rgba(31,26,35,0.68); --ink-mute: rgba(31,26,35,0.42); --cream-soft: #FBF6EE (card bg + cream text); --cream-warm/--mn-cream: #F4ECDF (confetti 5th color); --rule: rgba(31,26,35,0.10); dark hairline rgba(251,246,238,0.12).`
README's token list cites magenta #C44569 / butter #E8C465 / rose #D88B9F / cyan #59BFE7 / black #0A0A0F / ink #1F1A23 / cream #FBF6EE — consistent with the effective (overridden) palette; the only confetti caveat is the 5th color being #F4ECDF in code vs #FBF6EE in the README prose.

contactStack.contact_page: src/pages/contact.astro (28 lines). Static page (no `export const prerender` line, so it inherits the project default — prerendered/SSG). Uses MarketingLayout with inCard={false}, title="Get in touch — Innovative Medical Staffing". Renders SiteNav (slot="nav"), then a single <ContactPanel as="h1" /> (passes as="h1" so the panel headline is the page H1), then SiteFooter (slot="footer"). It renders NOTHING of its own beyond the panel — the entire contact surface IS the shared ContactPanel partial. The header comment (lines 2-14) explicitly states the prior page was a skeleton whose Turnstile/Resend form was "deferred and never wired," and flags (lines 11-13) that the form does a native POST to /api/contact but "the endpoint + Cloudflare Turnstile + token validation + the success-state UX are not yet wired into this design."
contactStack.contact_panel: src/components/sections/ContactPanel.astro (101 lines). Located in src/components/sections/. Reused on both the homepage (inline, as h2 default) and /contact (as h1). Single Prop `as?: 'h1' | 'h2'` (default 'h2') controlling only the headline tag. Markup: <section class="section section--cream contact-b-section" id="contact"> with two decorative glow divs, a left column (eyebrow "Get in touch", headline "Let's talk.", sub copy, and a <dl class="contact-b__info"> listing Email/Phone/Office), and the form. Form fields (lines 69-78): text input name="name" (required), email input name="email" (required), select name="audience" (required; options facility/clinician/other), text input name="role" (optional "Role or specialty"), textarea name="message". Submit is a styled <button type="submit"> ("Send it"). Classes come from the kit's kit.css .contact-b* styles; a scoped <style> block (lines 90-101) only adds the custom select chevron. Header comment notes zero-JS: the kit's client-side "sent" toggle was DROPPED in favor of a native POST, and reiterates /api/contact + Turnstile + success-state are NOT wired.
contactStack.form_post_target: ContactPanel.astro line 64: <form class="contact-b__form" method="post" action="/api/contact">. It is a plain native HTML form POST (no JS handler, no fetch, no preventDefault). There is NO Turnstile widget inside the form and NO hidden turnstileToken input rendered. On submit the browser navigates to /api/contact via a full-page POST. Because that endpoint does not exist (see below), the current real-world behavior is a 404 (or the SPA/worker 404 page) — the form is non-functional end-to-end. CSP permits the POST: src/middleware-logic.ts line 35 sets `form-action 'self'` (comment line 26 explicitly: "form-action 'self' allows POST to /api/contact + /api/apply").
contactStack.api_contact_endpoint: DOES NOT EXIST. Glob for src/pages/api/contact.* returns nothing. The only file in src/pages/api/ is locumsmart-webhook.ts (the LocumSmart job-webhook receiver — unrelated to the contact form). So nothing handles the form POST. A complete reference implementation of the endpoint — Turnstile siteverify + a Resend-backed transactional email with envOk() guard for RESEND_API_KEY/RESEND_FROM_ADDRESS/RECRUITING_TO_ADDRESS — exists ONLY as a spec in docs/plans/2026-05-06-ims-website-phase-1-plan.md (around lines 3277-3581: TURNSTILE_VERIFY_URL fetch, `new Resend(...)`, verifyTurnstile() gate). It was never ported into src/pages/api/. Additionally `resend` is NOT an installed dependency (package.json has @supabase/supabase-js ^2.105.4 and astro ^5.18.1, but no resend), so even the email-send half cannot run without adding the dep. Note: the endpoint would also need `export const prerender = false` to run on the Cloudflare worker (the SSR path used by locumsmart-webhook.ts / the /jobs page).
contactStack.turnstile: src/components/forms/TurnstileWidget.astro (61 lines) EXISTS and is well-built, but is NEVER USED anywhere — grep for "TurnstileWidget" returns only the file's own definition; it is not imported by contact.astro, ContactPanel.astro, or any other page/component. It reads the site key from import.meta.env.PUBLIC_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY (line 2), takes Props { form: string (CSS selector of target form), instanceId?: string }, renders a <div class="cf-turnstile" data-sitekey data-form data-callback> and loads https://challenges.cloudflare.com/turnstile/v0/api.js. A per-widget inline callback (sanitized JS-identifier name, lines 16/30-60) finds the widget by data-callback, locates the target form via data-form, and writes the token into a hidden input[name="turnstileToken"] (creating it if absent). Security hardening folded in (Codex r2): `form` deliberately kept out of define:vars due to Astro 5.18 lacking the GHSA-j687-52p2-xcff patch. Env vars PUBLIC_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY + TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY are referenced only in the spec/plan docs (docs/specs/...design.md lines 91-92; plan lines 3044-3045, 3072) — not in any committed .env/.env.example in the repo. CSP already allowlists Turnstile: script-src + connect-src + frame-src include https://challenges.cloudflare.com (middleware-logic.ts lines 29, 33, 34). Net: the widget component is ready but orphaned — to wire it the ContactPanel form would need to import and render <TurnstileWidget form=".contact-b__form" /> so the hidden turnstileToken input gets populated for the server to verify.
contactStack.success_handling: NONE. There is no success/confirmation state of any kind today. The kit's original client-side "sent" toggle was intentionally dropped (per ContactPanel header comment) in the zero-JS port. The form is a plain native POST with no redirect target, no inline success message, no aria-live region, and no thank-you page. Because /api/contact does not exist, a real submission currently yields a 404 rather than any confirmation. A working success path (e.g. POST → 303 redirect to a /contact?sent=1 or /thank-you page, or a server-rendered success view) still needs to be designed and built.
contactStack.real_contact_details: Real details are consistent everywhere and were landed in the 2026-05-28 content pass. Email recruiting@iastaffing.com, Phone (512) 524-6686 (tel:+15125246686), Location Fort Worth, TX. Appearances: ContactPanel.astro (email line 43, phone line 50, office line 57) with service-claim meta "Replies within one business day" (line 44) and "Mon to Fri, 8a to 6p Central" (line 51) — both flagged in the header comment as claims Zach should confirm. SiteFooter.astro "Find us" column (email line 56, phone line 57, Fort Worth line 58; copyright line 65). LegalPage.astro SUPPORT_EMAIL constant (line 23) + "Innovative Medical Staffing · Fort Worth, TX" mailing address (lines 78, 118, 137). facilities.astro phone CTAs (lines 24, 187-189). about.astro "since 2018 · Fort Worth, TX" / "47 states" framing (lines 7, 18, 128, 140). contactStack.whats_wired_vs_not:
WORKS end-to-end today: (1) Contact page renders — /contact mounts ContactPanel with correct H1, nav, footer. (2) Real, consistent contact details displayed across ContactPanel, footer, JSON-LD, legal pages, facilities. (3) Direct-contact channels are fully functional: mailto: and tel: links work, so users can email/call without the form. (4) CSP is already provisioned for the form + Turnstile (form-action 'self'; cloudflare turnstile allowlisted in script/connect/frame-src). (5) TurnstileWidget component is built and security-hardened.
STUBBED / MISSING for a working contact form: (1) src/pages/api/contact.ts does NOT exist — the form POSTs into a void (404). (2) `resend` dependency NOT installed — no email transport. (3) Env vars not provisioned in repo: RESEND_API_KEY / RESEND_FROM_ADDRESS / RECRUITING_TO_ADDRESS / PUBLIC_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEY / TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY (referenced only in spec/plan docs + the Pages dashboard checklist). (4) Turnstile NOT wired into the form — TurnstileWidget is never imported/rendered, so no <div class="cf-turnstile"> and no hidden input[name="turnstileToken"] exist on the page; server-side siteverify therefore can't be enforced. (5) NO success/confirmation UX (no redirect, no inline message, no thank-you page) — the kit's client "sent" toggle was removed. (6) Endpoint would need `export const prerender = false` to execute on the Cloudflare worker. A full reference implementation (Turnstile verify + Resend send) exists only in docs/plans/2026-05-06-ims-website-phase-1-plan.md (~lines 3277-3581) and was never ported to source.

tokens.color_vars:
Brand colors live in TWO active layers plus a legacy file.

ACTIVE LAYER 1 — src/styles/colors_and_type.css :root (Chromia-Cream base):
- Cream system: --cream #F6EFE8, --cream-soft #FBF6EE, --cream-warm #F4ECDF, --bone #F4F2E7
- Ink system: --ink #1F1A23, --ink-soft rgba(31,26,35,0.68), --ink-mute rgba(31,26,35,0.42), --ink-emphasis #C44569
- Pop accents: --pop-coral #F0846B, --pop-rose #E891B0, --pop-butter #F2C84B, --pop-mint #7AC9A8, --pop-sky #7BB8DC, --pop-lilac #B5A0E8, --pop-magenta #D14B6E
- Soft variants: --soft-coral #FBD8C8, --soft-rose #F8DCE7, --soft-butter #FAEBB8, --soft-mint #D2EFE0, --soft-sky #D2E5F2, --soft-lilac #E0D6F5
- IMS logo cyan (isolated to logo): --ims-cyan #59BFE7
- Lines/shadows: --rule rgba(31,26,35,0.10), --rule-strong rgba(31,26,35,0.20)

ACTIVE LAYER 2 — src/styles/palette-active.css :root (Magenta Noir, loaded LAST, wins). This is the real source of truth. It REDEFINES the pop/soft/cream tokens with !important AND adds a clean canonical --mn-* family:
- --mn-magenta #C44569, --mn-black #0A0A0F, --mn-butter #E8C465, --mn-rose #D88B9F, --mn-cyan #59BFE7, --mn-cream #F4ECDF
- It also force-overrides the base tokens: --pop-coral->#C44569, --pop-rose->#D88B9F, --pop-butter->#E8C465, --pop-mint->#0A0A0F (black!), --pop-sky->#2A2A2F, --pop-lilac->#3A2D33, --pop-magenta->#C44569, --cream->#F4ECDF, plus --soft-* desaturated grays/pinks (--soft-butter #F2E5B8, --soft-mint #D4D2D5, etc.).

So: YES to magenta (#C44569 as --mn-magenta / --pop-magenta / --pop-coral / --ink-emphasis), YES butter (--mn-butter #E8C465), YES rose (--mn-rose #D88B9F), YES cyan (--mn-cyan / --ims-cyan #59BFE7), YES black (--mn-black #0A0A0F), YES ink (--ink #1F1A23), YES cream (--mn-cream / --cream #F4ECDF after override).

LEGACY (src/styles/tokens.css) is the retired dark-navy system (--ims-navy-900 #0B132A, --card-tangerine #FF8C3A, etc.) — kept only for verify-build assertions and NOT loaded on the Chromia-Cream marketing pages. Ignore for brand work.
tokens.token_mapping:
Map handoff README token -> existing var (canonical = --mn-* in palette-active.css). All values MATCH exactly:

- --magenta #C44569 -> --mn-magenta #C44569 (EXACT). Also surfaced as --pop-magenta, --pop-coral (both force-set to #C44569 via !important in palette-active.css), and --ink-emphasis #C44569 (declared in colors_and_type.css base — note base --pop-magenta is #D14B6E but is overridden to #C44569 in palette-active.css).
- --butter #E8C465 -> --mn-butter #E8C465 (EXACT). Also --pop-butter (force-set #E8C465; base value was #F2C84B). Soft surface variant: --soft-butter #F2E5B8 (README's stated soft butter #F2E5B8 matches exactly).
- --rose #D88B9F -> --mn-rose #D88B9F (EXACT). Also --pop-rose (force-set #D88B9F; base value was #E891B0).
- --cyan #59BFE7 -> --mn-cyan #59BFE7 (EXACT). Also --ims-cyan #59BFE7 in base (EXACT). Reserved to ONE role: confirmation/success/today/checkmarks.
- --black #0A0A0F -> --mn-black #0A0A0F (EXACT). NOTE: there is NO base token for this — true black only exists in palette-active.css. Curiously --pop-mint is force-overridden to #0A0A0F (the dark sections reuse the old "mint" class names but render black).
- --ink #1F1A23 -> --ink #1F1A23 (EXACT, colors_and_type.css). This is deep plum-black for primary type/CTAs, distinct from --mn-black #0A0A0F used for dark section grounding.
- --cream #FBF6EE -> CLOSEST is --cream-soft #FBF6EE (EXACT match in colors_and_type.css), used for cards/modals. MISMATCH on the page-bg token: --mn-cream and the overridden --cream are #F4ECDF (not #FBF6EE). README's #FBF6EE corresponds to --cream-soft, while the page background is the warmer #F4ECDF. So "cream" splits into two: page bg = #F4ECDF (--mn-cream / --cream), card/soft cream = #FBF6EE (--cream-soft).

RECOMMENDATION for new code: use the --mn-* family (--mn-magenta/--mn-black/--mn-butter/--mn-rose/--mn-cyan/--mn-cream) — palette-active.css explicitly comments "Active palette tokens (use these in new code)." For soft card surfaces use --cream-soft (#FBF6EE) and --soft-butter (#F2E5B8).
tokens.type_families:
Defined in src/styles/colors_and_type.css :root (active). Self-hosted to satisfy CSP font-src 'self'.

- Display serif: --font-display: "Battlefin", "Fraunces Variable", "Fraunces", "Migra", Georgia, serif. Battlefin (Pangram Pangram, paid, self-hosted /fonts/) is the primary display face, upright-ONLY at weights 700 + 900 (two @font-face blocks: Battlefin-Bold woff2/woff/ttf = 700, Battlefin-Black = 900). Headings h1/h2 use weight 900; h3/h4 use 700.
- Display italic: --font-display-italic: "Fraunces Variable", "Fraunces", "Migra", Georgia, serif. Because Battlefin is upright-only, italic emphasis falls through to Fraunces (NOT synthetic italic — font-synthesis:none on headings). Fraunces is imported as an @fontsource package in MarketingLayout.astro.
- Body sans: --font-body: "Inter Variable", "Inter", system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif. Inter variable via @fontsource. Body weights: 400 body, 600 eyebrows/labels/h5.
- Mono: --font-mono: "JetBrains Mono", ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, monospace. Used for .mono, badges, eyebrow micro-labels, stat captions, calendar mocks.

NOTE the emphasis convention: headlines do NOT swap font or italicize for the accent word — single typeface, one word recolored to --ink-emphasis (#C44569) via h_ em / .em hooks.

Legacy aliases for verify-build parity also exist: --font (Inter stack) and TAY Basal / TAY Amaya @font-face blocks are declared but NOT loaded by default. tokens.css (legacy dark system) defines its own --font/--font-sans/--font-mono around Inter only — not used on Chromia-Cream pages.

tokens.hairlines:
Hairline/border conventions (src/styles/colors_and_type.css + kit.css):

ON CREAM surfaces (ink-on-light): hairlines are ink at low alpha.
- --rule: rgba(31,26,35,0.10) — standard hairline (footer bottom border, input--light border, card borders, newsletter input).
- --rule-strong: rgba(31,26,35,0.20) — emphasized rule (break ornament, link underline default text-decoration-color).
- Section-anchor uses a heavier 1.5px SOLID --ink bottom border (border-bottom: 1.5px solid var(--ink)) as a strong divider.
- Inline ad-hoc hairlines on cream also appear as rgba(31,26,35,0.06)/0.08 (compare item borders, divider).
- Secondary pill border: 1.5px solid var(--ink).

ON DARK / ink surfaces (--ink #1F1A23 or --mn-black #0A0A0F panels): hairlines flip to cream at low alpha.
- rgba(251,246,238,0.1)–0.18 (the cream-soft #FBF6EE channel): contact .input border rgba(251,246,238,0.18), compare__col--ims item borders rgba(251,246,238,0.1), positioning statchip dividers rgba(251,246,238,0.15), nav link text rgba(251,246,238,0.72).

Link underlines: 1px thickness, text-underline-offset 3px, color --rule-strong, shifting to --ink-aubergine (mapped to --pop-magenta) on hover.

NOTE: the legacy dark tokens.css uses white-alpha hairlines instead (--border rgba(255,255,255,0.05), --hairline #2E3D63) — a DIFFERENT convention, but that file is not loaded on Chromia-Cream marketing pages. On dark Chromia-Cream sections the hairline base is the warm cream #FBF6EE alpha, not pure white.

motion.scroll_reveal_component:
FILE: c:/Users/oclou/QueenClaude/ias-website/.worktrees/feat-ims-phase-1-plan/src/components/util/ScrollReveal.astro

PROPS (interface Props, lines 2-10):
- `as?: string` — wrapper tag, default 'section' (rendered as `<As>`).
- `delay?: number` — reveal delay in ms, default 0.
- `class?: string` — extra classes, merged via class:list with 'scroll-reveal'.
- `[key: string]: unknown` — all other attributes (id, aria-*, role, data-*) forwarded via {...rest}.

RENDER (line 13): `<As class:list={['scroll-reveal', className]} data-reveal-delay={delay} {...rest}><slot /></As>`

CSS (scoped <style>, lines 17-38):
- `.scroll-reveal` default: opacity:0; transform:translateY(12px); transition opacity+transform 500ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); will-change: opacity, transform.
- `.scroll-reveal.is-revealed`: opacity:1; transform:translateY(0).
- reduced-motion guard (lines 31-37): forces opacity:1; transform:none; transition:none.
NOTE: the slide distance here is 12px / 500ms. This is DIFFERENT from the page-local `.reveal` class (40px / 720ms) found in kit.css and about/facilities — two unrelated reveal systems coexist.

JS (module <script>, lines 40-78) — this is BUNDLED (Astro processes <script>), served from self, CSP-clean:
- `revealAll()` adds `.is-revealed` to every `.scroll-reveal`.
- Computes `prefersReducedMotion` via matchMedia.
- FALLBACK BRANCH (line 54): if `window` undefined OR no IntersectionObserver OR prefersReducedMotion -> revealAll() synchronously. This is the explicit guard against the opacity:0 default trapping content hidden.
- OTHERWISE: IntersectionObserver with `rootMargin: '-10%'`; on intersect reads `dataset.revealDelay`, applies setTimeout if >0, adds `.is-revealed`, then `observer.unobserve(el)` (once-only).

OPACITY:0 RISK ASSESSMENT: The component is self-protecting for the supported runtime paths — the inline <script> reveals synchronously when IO is missing or reduced-motion is set, and the CSS @media reduced-motion guard sets opacity:1 independently of JS. HOWEVER the risk is real if the bundled script fails to load/execute at all (CSP blocks the bundle, JS disabled, or a JS error earlier on the page halts module eval) AND the user does NOT have prefers-reduced-motion set: in that case `.scroll-reveal` stays at opacity:0 permanently with NO no-JS CSS fallback (there is no `<noscript>` rule and no non-reduced-motion safety net in the scoped CSS). Given this repo's documented history of CSP blocking inline scripts (per project memory, Wave 2 dynamism was CSP-blocked), this is the one fragility worth flagging. Mitigation if expanding usage: add a CSS fallback (e.g. reveal after an animation/keyframe, or a `.no-js`/`html:not(.js)` rule) so first paint is never permanently blank without JS.
motion.existing_css_animations:
All motion lives in c:/Users/oclou/QueenClaude/ias-website/.worktrees/feat-ims-phase-1-plan/src/styles/. Files: tokens.css, colors_and_type.css, kit.css, palette-active.css. Motion is concentrated in kit.css; tokens.css/palette-active.css define easing/duration tokens only.

GLOBAL REDUCED-MOTION KILL-SWITCH — colors_and_type.css:389-398: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration:0.01ms!important; animation-iteration-count:1!important; transition-duration:0.01ms!important; scroll-behavior:auto!important; } }`. This is the site-wide blanket guard: it neutralizes EVERY animation/transition regardless of per-component guards. So any new transition/animation is automatically reduced-motion-safe even without its own guard (though several add explicit guards too).

@KEYFRAMES (all in kit.css):
- blobFloatA/B/C (294/298/302) — used by .hero blobs (276/284/292, 12-16s infinite) and by about/clinicians/facilities `.atmosphere` blobs (each page has its own reduced-motion guard: about.astro:175, clinicians.astro:267, facilities.astro:~).
- scrollBob (346) — .hero__scroll-arrow 1.8s infinite; guarded kit.css:351-353 (`.hero__scroll-arrow { animation:none; }`).
- pulse (600) `{0%,100%{opacity:1}50%{opacity:0.4}}` — reused by .role-card__dot (596) and another dot (1671); .role-card__dot guarded at 1686.
- chipFloatA-D (994-997) — .sticky-chip--* (989-993, 8-12s infinite); guarded kit.css:999 (`.sticky-chip{animation:none!important}`).
- heroWordCycle (1038) — .hero__rotator-word 9.6s infinite with staggered animation-delay 0/2.4/4.8/7.2s (1034-1037); guarded 1045-1046. THIS is the homepage hero's rotating-word effect (Personal/Curated/Thoughtful/Human), CSS-only, no JS.
- marqueeScroll (1086) `translateX(0)->translateX(-50%)` — .marquee__track 32s linear infinite; guarded 1087. Speed overridden by body[data-vibe] (cozy 56s / loud 18s, 2033/2045).
- ctaPopBob (1189) — .cta-pop var duration ~10s infinite; guarded TWICE (duplicate) 1293 + 1295.

KEY TRANSITIONS (kit.css): topnav show/hide transform+opacity 640/480ms + will-change (141-142); pill/pill__arrow/pill__puck hover transforms (51/78/98/198); card hovers transform+box-shadow var(--dur-base) (448/543/669/1100/1547/1636); proc-mock/cand-card/cal-mock micro-transitions (1373-1375); aud-mix card transition guarded off at reduced-motion 1688.

SCROLL-REVEAL CSS CLASSES — TWO SYSTEMS:
1. `.scroll-reveal` (12px/500ms) — scoped INSIDE ScrollReveal.astro only; JS-driven (.is-revealed), own reduced-motion guard.
2. `.reveal` / `.reveal.in` (40px/720ms) — kit.css:1517-1525 (comment says "Applied via JS that adds .reveal then .in") with reduced-motion guard 1523-1524. DUPLICATED locally in about.astro:372-374 and facilities.astro:693-700. CRITICAL: there is NO JavaScript anywhere in src/ that adds the `.in` class (grep for classList.add('in') / IntersectionObserver in pages = zero matches), AND no element carries class="reveal". So `.reveal` is entirely DEAD/orphaned CSS — defining a reveal mechanism that is never wired up. Any new scroll-reveal work should standardize on the working `.scroll-reveal` component, not resurrect `.reveal`.
motion.pages_using_reveal:
CONFIRMED — pages/components that USE ScrollReveal.astro (via Grep on import + usage):

DIRECTLY IN PAGES (only 2, matching the expectation):
- src/pages/how-it-works.astro — imports at line 23; wraps all 3 sections: #hero (102), #process (114), #closing (159).
- src/pages/specialties.astro — imports at line 18; wraps all 3 sections: #hero (89), #grid (101), #closing (121).

ALSO USED (component layer, but see caveat below):
- src/components/sections/Home*.astro ALL import and wrap with ScrollReveal: HomeHero (delay={0}), HomePositioningStrip, HomeAudienceSplit, HomeProcess, HomeSpecialties, HomeValues, HomeConcierge, HomePhilosophy, HomeHumanLedTech, HomeFinalCta.

CRITICAL CAVEAT — these Home* components are DEAD CODE. Grep of src/pages for HomeHero|HomeProcess|... returns ZERO usages in any page (only doc-comment mentions in specialties.astro:8 and how-it-works.astro:4,8,14). The live homepage src/pages/index.astro does NOT import or use ANY Home* component — it hand-rolls raw `<section>` blocks inline and only imports MarqueeStrip + ContactPanel. So the Home* + ScrollReveal section set is an unused/superseded alternate implementation.

PAGES THAT DO NOT USE ScrollReveal (all use plain `<section>`, no reveal wiring):
- src/pages/index.astro (homepage) — raw sections, NO reveal at all.
- src/pages/about.astro — raw sections; defines dead `.reveal` CSS but no class usage, no JS.
- src/pages/clinicians.astro — raw sections, no reveal.
- src/pages/facilities.astro — raw sections; defines dead `.reveal` CSS (693-700) but no class usage, no JS.
- src/pages/jobs/index.astro — raw sections, no reveal (has its own filter JS instead).
- src/pages/contact.astro, privacy.astro, terms.astro, cookies.astro — not inspected in detail but no ScrollReveal import (grep covered all of src/).

NET: scroll-reveal motion is currently live on exactly TWO secondary pages (how-it-works, specialties). The 5 primary marketing pages in this task (index, about, clinicians, facilities, jobs) have NO entrance-reveal motion today — they rely only on CSS hover transitions + ambient blob/marquee keyframes.

Top-level content units in document order for each target page. All pages share the pattern: SiteNav (slot=nav, fixed topnav) + content + SiteFooter (slot=footer). Hero/above-fold flagged [ABOVE-FOLD — first-paint] = must render visible at first paint (reveal it with delay=0 or skip reveal so it is never opacity:0 on load).

1. HERO — `<section class="section section--cream hero hero--center">` #hero-headline (line 74). [ABOVE-FOLD — first-paint]. Contains CSS-only rotating word + decorative blobs (aria-hidden). Do NOT default to opacity:0.

=== src/pages/jobs/index.astro — raw <section> inside .variation, no reveal; SSR Supabase, has own filter JS ===
1. `.jb__hero` `<section class="jb__hero">` (147). [ABOVE-FOLD — first-paint]. Contains eyebrow count + title + the search form (#jobs-search-form) + specialty chips (#jobs-chips). NOTE: interactive controls live here — keep visible/interactive at first paint.
2. `.jb-section` `<section class="jb-section">` (196). Holds #jobs-grid (the card grid, server-rendered from {jobs.map()}) + #jobs-empty empty-state.
Reveal unit candidates: section 2 (the grid). Hero (1) above-fold AND interactive — do not hide. CAUTION: cards are filtered live via the page's bundled <script> (toggles .is-hidden); any reveal mechanism must not conflict with .is-hidden display toggling (a transform/opacity reveal on cards that can also be display:none is fragile — IntersectionObserver re-show after filter changes would need care).
motion.stagger_candidates:
Repeating-item containers per page that are natural fits for STAGGERED child reveals (reveal each child with an incrementing delay). The current ScrollReveal.astro supports per-element `delay` (data-reveal-delay -> setTimeout), so staggering = wrapping each child as its own ScrollReveal with delay = index*step, OR adding a sibling-index stagger mechanism. Cards already have hover transitions on transform/box-shadow — a reveal that also animates transform should reset cleanly to translateY(0) so hover still works.

=== index.astro (homepage) ===
- POSITIONING stats row: `.positioning` containing 4 `.statchip` (lines 117-124). 4-item stat row — classic stagger candidate.
- AUDIENCE SPLIT: `.aud-mix` containing 2 `.aud-mix__card`; each card has an inner `.aud-mix__roles` list of 3 `.role-card`. Stagger the 2 cards, optionally the role-cards.
- COMPARE: two `.compare__list` each with 7 `.compare__item` (236-257) — long lists; row-by-row stagger reads well but 7+7 items may be too many; consider staggering the 2 columns only.
- PROCESS: `.process-cards` with 3 `.proc-card` (312-368) — ideal 3-up stagger.
- SPECIALTY GRID: `.spec-grid` with 8 `.spec-card` (385-397, {specialties.map}) — strong stagger candidate (cap delay so last card isn't too late).
- VALUES: `.values` with 3 `.value-card` (413-420) — 3-up stagger.

=== about.astro ===
- PILLARS: `.au__pillars-grid` with 3 `.au__pillar` (40-71) — 3-up stagger.
- TEAM: TWO grids — `.au__team-grid--3` Founders (3 `.au__member`, 87-106) and Leadership (3 `.au__member`, 111-130). Stagger members within each group.
- STATS: `.au__stats-grid` with 4 stat cells (141-158) — 4-item stat row stagger.

=== clinicians.astro ===
- TIMELINE: `.cc__timeline-track` with 4 `.cc__step` (31-85) — sequential timeline is the single best stagger candidate on the site (top-to-bottom 01->04 delay reads as the process unfolding).
- WHY: `.cc__why-grid` with 4 `.cc__why-card` (99-120) — 4-up stagger.
- QUOTES: `.cc__quotes-grid` with 3 `.cc__quote` (133-164) — 3-up stagger.

=== facilities.astro ===
- HERO ROSTER (above-fold, treat carefully): `.fb__roster` with 4 `.fb__roster-item` (28-62). Could stagger but it is above-fold — only stagger with delay=0 baseline.
- PILLARS: `.fb__pillars-grid` with 3 `.fb__pillar` (98-129) — 3-up stagger.
- QUOTES: `.fc__quotes-grid` with 3 `.fc__quote` (142-173) — 3-up stagger.

=== jobs/index.astro ===
- CARD GRID: `.jb-b__grid` (#jobs-grid) with N `.jb-b__card` ({jobs.map}, 198-220) — naturally a stagger candidate, BUT high-risk: cards are live-filtered by the page script toggling `.is-hidden` (display:none) and counts are recomputed. A staggered reveal must (a) not fight the display toggle, (b) handle re-filtering (cards re-shown after a filter change would need re-observing or a non-IO approach), and (c) the card count is dynamic/unbounded so a fixed per-index delay could trail off — cap the max delay. Recommend a simple whole-grid reveal or CSS-only first-paint stagger here rather than per-card IO reveal.

GENERAL STAGGER NOTE: how-it-works.astro `.steps` (4 `.step` li per panel) and specialties.astro `.grid` (12 specialty `.card` li) are also repeating containers — these pages already use section-level ScrollReveal but do NOT stagger their children, so they are the lowest-risk place to prototype child-stagger since the reveal plumbing is already proven there.
